source_alignment.subject: English
source_alignment.topic: Grammar choices for clarity and cohesion
source_alignment.source_statement: Choose nouns or pronouns appropriately for clarity and cohesion and to avoid repetition; use conjunctions, adverbs and prepositions to express time and cause.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This review-stage sample focuses on purposeful grammar choices: selecting nouns, pronouns and linking language that make Year 4 sentences and short paragraphs clear without unnecessary repetition.

objective.child_goal: I can choose words that make my writing clear and connected.
objective.statement: Choose nouns, pronouns and time-or-cause linking language to maintain clarity, avoid confusing repetition and build cohesion across sentences.
misconceptions: Replaces every repeated noun with a pronoun even when the reader cannot tell who or what it means.
Repeats the same noun in every sentence instead of using a clear pronoun or noun phrase.
Chooses a conjunction, adverb or preposition because it sounds impressive rather than because it matches the relationship.
Uses a pronoun that agrees with the wrong noun or changes the meaning.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A word such as he, she, it, they or them that can stand in for a noun.
vocabulary.audio_script: A pronoun can replace a noun, but it must be clear who or what it means.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A name card passes a glowing label to a pronoun card.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A noun with words that belong with it, such as 'the silver robot'.
vocabulary.audio_script: A noun phrase can remind the reader exactly which person, place or thing is meant.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A noun card with useful detail tiles clipped beside it.

vocabulary.child_explanation: How writing sticks together so the reader can follow it.
vocabulary.audio_script: Cohesion means the ideas in your writing connect clearly.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Sentence stepping stones linked by safe bridges.

vocabulary.audio_script: Cause language explains why something happened.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A because bridge from a reason card to a result card.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Who does 'she' mean in this sentence pair?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate pronoun reference before editing for cohesion.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A clear pronoun points back to one person, place or thing.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Pronoun threads connect to possible noun cards, with unclear threads shown as tangled.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Choose whether the next sentence needs 'it', 'the machine' or 'the tiny motor'.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce grammar choices as decisions that help the reader follow meaning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Sometimes a pronoun is smooth. Sometimes a noun phrase is clearer.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A clarity meter compares three replacement cards in a short paragraph.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Maya gave Aisha the map because she trusted her. Who trusted whom?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model spotting ambiguous pronouns and repairing them with a noun or noun phrase.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: If a pronoun could point to more than one person, use a name or noun phrase to make it clear.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two character cards both tug on 'she' until one is replaced with a name.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Choose the bridge word that shows why the team stopped.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise choosing conjunctions, adverbs or prepositions for time and cause relationships.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Ask whether the link shows time, cause, order or contrast before choosing it.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Sentence bridges are labelled time, cause and contrast; only the matching bridge locks.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which edit avoids repeating 'the dragon' but keeps the meaning clear?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect over-repetition and unclear replacement errors.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Good cohesion is not just fewer repeated words. It must still be clear.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A repetition alarm quietens only when the replacement still points to the right noun.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Rex changed every noun to 'it'. Teach Rex when to keep a noun phrase.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation to secure purposeful noun and pronoun choices.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I made it shorter, but now I cannot tell what 'it' means. Can you help me choose?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion's paragraph becomes tangled, then clears as noun phrases return where needed.

manipulatives.purpose: Make grammar choices visible by linking pronouns to nouns, testing clarity and selecting time-or-cause bridges between sentences.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners connect pronoun threads, swap noun and pronoun cards, repair ambiguous references, and choose linking words that match the relationship between ideas.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard card selection, audio sentence playback, non-colour reference threads, reduced-choice mode, clear line spacing and reduced-motion static clarity checks.

manipulatives.purpose: Make the meaning of a linking choice explicit by contrasting time order with cause and effect.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners read two world-event panels, route them across a time or cause bridge, test two candidate linking words and keep the one that preserves the intended relationship.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard shortcuts select the relationship and linking word; single-switch scanning moves through time, cause, candidate words and confirm; static mode uses paired sentences above labelled time-or-cause columns; reduced-motion mode replaces bridge routing with instant line labels and reads the completed sentence aloud.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave pronoun reference and time/cause links after learners can explain whether a replacement improves clarity.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Identify which noun a pronoun clearly refers to in short sentence pairs.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: sentence pair + pronoun + two or three possible noun referents
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid names or contexts that rely on gender stereotypes; include singular they where contextually clear.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Replace unnecessary repeated nouns with clear pronouns without losing meaning.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: short paragraph with repeated noun + three repair choices
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Teacher review needed where more than one edit could be stylistically acceptable.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Repair pronouns that could refer to more than one noun by choosing a clearer noun or noun phrase.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: ambiguous sentence pair + replacement options + clarity explanation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep ambiguity deliberate and resolvable with one best edit.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Choose conjunctions, adverbs or prepositions that show time or cause relationships accurately.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two clauses or sentences + intended relationship + because/after/during/therefore style choices
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Check distractors are plausible but not equally correct in the given context.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain pronoun reference, noun-phrase clarity and time/cause link decisions through spaced review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: mixed quick item from pronoun, repetition repair or link-choice blueprints
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep retrieval text short enough for warm-up use and audio-first playback.

body.prompt: In this sentence, what does 'it' mean? The otter found a shell and carried it to the riverbank.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Ask what was carried.
The otter carried the shell.
question_variants.explanation: 'It' means the shell because the shell is the thing being carried.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "the shell". 'It' means the shell because the shell is the thing being carried.
feedback.repair: Ask what was carried. The otter carried the shell. Use that clue to repair unclear pronoun reference, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this pronoun cohesion choice, check the exact sound, word, sentence or text detail named in the prompt. The language evidence supports value: "the shell": 'It' means the shell because the shell is the thing being carried.
feedback.misconception_check: unclear pronoun reference
feedback.support_message: Choose by touch or keyboard, scan with a switch, use eye gaze, point or use AAC, or direct an adult to scribe or record the choice. Speech and handwriting are optional. There is no timer or penalty, and correct work stays for the retry.

body.prompt: Choose the clearest edit: The dragon opened the gate. The dragon stepped into the cave.
choices: The dragon opened the gate. It stepped into the cave.
The dragon opened the gate. The gate stepped into the cave.
The dragon opened the gate. They stepped into the cave.
The dragon opened the gate. The dragon the dragon stepped into the cave.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

expected_answer.value: The dragon opened the gate. It stepped into the cave.

hints: Use a pronoun only if the reader can tell who it means.
The dragon is the one stepping into the cave.
question_variants.explanation: 'It' avoids repeating 'the dragon' and is clear because only the dragon can step into the cave.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "The dragon opened the gate. It stepped into the cave.". 'It' avoids repeating 'the dragon' and is clear because only the dragon can step into the cave.
feedback.repair: Use a pronoun only if the reader can tell who it means. The dragon is the one stepping into the cave. Use that clue to repair over repeats noun, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this noun pronoun repair, check the exact sound, word, sentence or text detail named in the prompt. The language evidence supports value: "The dragon opened the gate. It stepped into the cave.": 'It' avoids repeating 'the dragon' and is clear because only the dragon can step into the cave.
feedback.misconception_check: over repeats noun
feedback.support_message: Choose by touch or keyboard, scan with a switch, use eye gaze, point or use AAC, or direct an adult to scribe or record the choice. Speech and handwriting are optional. There is no timer or penalty, and correct work stays for the retry.

body.prompt: Which edit makes the meaning clearest? Maya thanked Aisha because she had found the missing compass.
choices: Maya thanked Aisha because Aisha had found the missing compass.
Maya thanked Aisha because she had found the missing compass.
She thanked Aisha because Maya had found the missing compass.
Maya thanked her because she had found it.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

expected_answer.value: Maya thanked Aisha because Aisha had found the missing compass.

hints: There are two girls, so 'she' could be confusing.
Use the name of the person who found the compass.
question_variants.explanation: Repeating 'Aisha' is clearer here because it shows that Aisha found the compass.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "Maya thanked Aisha because Aisha had found the missing compass.". Repeating 'Aisha' is clearer here because it shows that Aisha found the compass.
feedback.repair: There are two girls, so 'she' could be confusing. Use the name of the person who found the compass. Use that clue to repair pronoun points to wrong noun, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this cohesion edit, check the exact sound, word, sentence or text detail named in the prompt. The language evidence supports value: "Maya thanked Aisha because Aisha had found the missing compass.": Repeating 'Aisha' is clearer here because it shows that Aisha found the compass.
feedback.misconception_check: pronoun points to wrong noun
feedback.support_message: Choose by touch or keyboard, scan with a switch, use eye gaze, point or use AAC, or direct an adult to scribe or record the choice. Speech and handwriting are optional. There is no timer or penalty, and correct work stays for the retry.

body.prompt: Choose the best link: The path was flooded, ___ the hikers chose a safer route.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: The second idea happens as a result.
Use the link that shows cause and effect.
question_variants.explanation: 'So' shows that the flooded path caused the hikers to choose a safer route.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "so". 'So' shows that the flooded path caused the hikers to choose a safer route.
feedback.repair: The second idea happens as a result. Use the link that shows cause and effect. Use that clue to repair linker without logic, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this time cause link choice, check the exact sound, word, sentence or text detail named in the prompt. The language evidence supports value: "so": 'So' shows that the flooded path caused the hikers to choose a safer route.
feedback.misconception_check: linker without logic
feedback.support_message: Choose by touch or keyboard, scan with a switch, use eye gaze, point or use AAC, or direct an adult to scribe or record the choice. Speech and handwriting are optional. There is no timer or penalty, and correct work stays for the retry.

body.prompt: Choose the best time phrase: ___ the science lesson, we tested which material was waterproof.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: The phrase tells when the testing happened.
Use the word that means 'in the time of'.
question_variants.explanation: 'During the science lesson' tells when the testing happened.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "During". 'During the science lesson' tells when the testing happened.
feedback.repair: The phrase tells when the testing happened. Use the word that means 'in the time of'. Use that clue to repair time cause confusion, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this time cause link choice, check the exact sound, word, sentence or text detail named in the prompt. The language evidence supports value: "During": 'During the science lesson' tells when the testing happened.
feedback.misconception_check: time cause confusion
feedback.support_message: Choose by touch or keyboard, scan with a switch, use eye gaze, point or use AAC, or direct an adult to scribe or record the choice. Speech and handwriting are optional. There is no timer or penalty, and correct work stays for the retry.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Replaces every repeated noun with a pronoun even when the reader cannot tell who or what it means.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses an edit where 'he', 'she', 'it' or 'they' could refer to more than one noun.
repair_sequence: List the possible nouns the pronoun could point to.
Ask whether the reader can choose one without guessing.
Replace the unclear pronoun with a name or precise noun phrase.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner repairs a new ambiguous pronoun and explains why the noun phrase is clearer.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Repeats the same noun in every sentence instead of using a clear pronoun or noun phrase.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner rejects all pronoun edits even when there is only one clear referent.
repair_sequence: Check that one clear referent is available.
Swap one repeat for a pronoun and reread for flow.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner chooses a pronoun that reduces repetition while keeping reference clear.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Chooses a conjunction, adverb or preposition because it sounds impressive rather than because it matches the relationship.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses 'although', 'therefore' or 'during' where the sentence needs a simple time or cause link.
repair_sequence: Name the relationship between the ideas.
Test the link as time, cause, order or contrast.
Reread the sentence and reject links that change the meaning.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner selects a link that matches the intended relationship and explains the choice.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use calm editing-workbench colours, no flashing error marks and optional plain-text mode.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use static pronoun threads and before-and-after edit cards instead of animated tangles.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Optional sentence-level narration uses only ElevenLabs assets after human listening approval. Browser TTS is prohibited; punctuation tasks use neutral or delayed playback when intonation could reveal the answer, and every item remains fully solvable from visible text.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Chunk short paragraphs into sentence cards with line focus, glossary popovers and dyslexia-friendly spacing.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Ask one decision at a time: who or what is meant, whether repetition helps or hurts, then which link fits.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow hide-and-compare choices, undo, oral rehearsal and explanation frames such as 'This pronoun means...'.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Frame edits as reader-help choices and reward clear explanations even when the first choice needs repair.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach pronoun, noun phrase, link, cause, time, clear and repeat with icons and paired oral examples.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires choosing clear noun or pronoun references and accurate time or cause links in short sentence pairs; secure requires repairing ambiguity and explaining how the grammar choice improves cohesion across a short paragraph.
evidence.parent_summary: Can choose nouns, pronouns and linking words that make writing clear and easy to follow.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Identifies pronoun referents, avoids confusing repetition, selects time and cause links accurately and justifies grammar choices by their effect on the reader.
next_actions: Teacher review of subjective cohesion edits before pilot.
Interleave with paragraph organisation and fronted adverbials after clarity choices are secure.
Add learner-writing application tasks once proof items are reviewed.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 4 grammar choices pack with a deterministic 260-item pilot bank and five preserved curated variants. The bank develops tense consistency, pronouns and determiners, punctuation, noun phrases, adverbials, Standard English, purposeful sentence choices and meaning effects alongside cohesion. Generated candidates include SEND and dyslexia scaffolds, optional human-reviewed ElevenLabs references with browser TTS prohibited, supported interactions, rich editing feedback and untimed editor missions. Independent English, teacher, accessibility, safeguarding, audio and renderer review remain required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
